name: [2511.18417] Categorical Equivariant Deep Learning
snippet:
2025 · Cited by 3 — Formulating linear and nonlinear layers in the categorical setup , we prove the equivariant universal approximation theorem in the general ... Read more
We develop a theory of category-equivariant neural networks (CENNs) that unifies group/groupoid-equivariant networks, poset/lattice-equivariant networks, graph and sheaf neural networks.

name: Universal Approximation Theorem for Equivariant Maps by
snippet:
Cited by 23 — This work studies the question of universal approximation with neural networks under general symmetries . For this purpose, the authors first leverage ... Read more
This paper provides a unified method to obtain universal approximation theorems for equivariant maps by CNNs in various settings. As its significant advantage, we can handle non-linear equivariant maps between infinite-dimensional spaces for non-compact groups.

name: Equivariant Universal Approximation Theorem
snippet:
30 Nov 2025 — It formulates data and symmetries via topological categories, feature functors, and Radon measures, using convolutional layers and nonpolynomial ... Read more
The Equivariant Universal Approximation Theorem generalizes classical universal approximation results to the setting where neural networks are required to respect symmetries encoded by equivariance under categorical, group-theoretic, or combinatorial structures.

name: Theory for Equivariant Quantum Neural Networks
snippet:
2024 · Cited by 178 — A comprehensive theoretical framework to design equivariant quantum neural networks (EQNNs) for essentially any relevant symmetry group . Read more
In this work, we import these ideas to the quantum realm by presenting a comprehensive theoretical framework to design equivariant quantum neural networks (EQNNs) for essentially any relevant symmetry group.

name: Categorical Equivariant Deep Learning: Category-Equivariant
snippet: A unified mathematical framework for deep learning, called Category-Equivariant Neural Networks (CENNs), is presented to encompass diverse data symmetries beyond group actions using category theory.
